item.name: Demo Scenarios
item.description:
Three demo scenarios that showcase the RAG pipeline, matching the README data flow cases:

1. **Case 1: Definition Only** — Partial RAG match + LLM fallback from trained knowledge.
2. **Case 2: Simple Data Question** — RAG for definitions + SQL for data, combined by the agent.
3. **Case 3: Complex Report** — Full hybrid: 3 SQL queries + RAG context + LLM narrative synthesis.

item.name: Case 1: Definition Only (Partial RAG + LLM Fallback)
item.description:
The question asks for a general definition of 'enrollment' and its types.
The knowledge base only has 'Enrollment Rate' (a metric), NOT a general
definition. RAG returns partial matches; Claude fills the gaps from its
trained knowledge.

Run 1a first to see the full agent answer, then run 1b to see what RAG
actually found (partial matches with high distances).

item.name: 1a. Ask: What is enrollment and its types? (agent answer)
1b. Search: What RAG actually has about enrollment (raw chunks)
Case 2: Simple Data Question (RAG + SQL + LLM)
item.description:
A compound question: definition (RAG) + data (SQL).

The agent calls rag_search_tool for 'enrollment rate' definition (strong
match in glossary, distance ~0.31) AND sql_query_tool to find which
campaign has the most enrollments. Both results are combined into one answer.

Run 2a for the definition only, 2b for data only, 2c for both combined.

item.name: 2a. Ask: What is enrollment rate? (definition via RAG)
2b. Ask SQL: Which campaign has the highest enrollment? (data via SQL)
2c. Ask: Combined — definition + data in one question (agent uses both tools)
Case 3: Complex Report Request (RAG + SQL + LLM)
item.description:
The most complex flow using performance_summary_tool.

CMP-003 (Spring Dining Deal) is used because it has the best story:
highest redemption rate (74%), student-focused, smallest budget ($100K)
yet highest ROI (210%), mobile-dominant channel.

The tool internally runs 3 SQL queries + 1 RAG search, assembles
everything into an augmented prompt, and has Claude write a 3-4
paragraph narrative report.

Run 3a-3c to see the individual pieces, then 3d for the full report.

item.name: 3a. Get: Campaign details for CMP-003 (raw data)
3b. Search: RAG context for CMP-003 (knowledge base chunks)
3c. Ask SQL: Monthly performance trend for CMP-003 (raw SQL data)
3d. Get Summary: Full AI-generated performance report for CMP-003
